sanskrit: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः । छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
hindiTranslation: जिनका शरीर मनबुद्धिइन्द्रियोंसहित वशमें है, जो सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके हितमें रत हैं, जिनके सम्पूर्ण संशय मिट गये हैं, जिनके सम्पूर्ण कल्मष दोष नष्ट हो गये हैं, वे विवेकी साधक निर्वाण ब्रह्मको प्राप्त होते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Sages who are free of impurity, free of doubt, self-controlled, and devoted to the welfare of all beings attain freedom in the supreme reality.
essence: Freedom arrives when doubt ends and life turns toward all beings.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who have cleansed themselves, ended doubt, controlled their minds and senses, and care for the good of all beings reach freedom in the supreme reality.
simpleInsight: Inner freedom feels possible when self-control and care for others become one practice.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still answering Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, where the warrior has collapsed under doubt. After describing the calm, inwardly rooted sage, he adds the outward sign: such a person lives for the welfare of all beings and reaches freedom in the supreme reality.
modernRelevance: You’re standing outside a hospital room after a hard diagnosis, holding back panic so you can help your family. Clear action begins when doubt settles and concern widens beyond yourself.
translationLiteral: लभन्ते (attain) / ब्रह्मनिर्वाणम् (freedom in the supreme reality) / ऋषयः (sages) / क्षीणकल्मषाः (whose impurities are diminished) / छिन्नद्वैधाः (whose doubts are cut away) / यतात्मानः (self-controlled) / सर्वभूतहिते (in the welfare of all beings) / रताः (delighting, devoted)
